name: Synthetic Student Response Model-Choice and Student-Simulation Validity Search

search_queries.query: synthetic student responses LLM model comparison GPT-4 GPT-3.5 generated student answers education paper
search_queries.notes: Used to identify off-the-shelf LLM comparison and student-simulation validity papers.

search_queries.query: LLM simulated students model comparison student responses education arXiv
search_queries.notes: Used to identify recent arXiv simulated-student papers.

search_queries.query: large language models simulate student responses model comparison generated answers educational assessment
search_queries.notes: Used to identify education and assessment simulation studies.

search_queries.query: synthetic student response generation model comparison prompt student answers scoring
search_queries.notes: Used to screen model and prompt effects for synthetic responses.

search_queries.query: 2605.12748 Simulating Students or Sycophantic Problem Solving
2601.05473 Towards Valid Student Simulation with Large Language Models
2025.bea-1.8 Can LLMs Effectively Simulate Human Learners

inclusion_criteria: Primary source with full text or authoritative abstract/metadata available.
Addresses LLM-based student simulation, generated student responses, or learner behavior emulation.
Adds evidence about model choice, prompt-only limits, realism/fidelity evaluation, or reporting criteria for synthetic student responses.
Helps refine the application memo without requiring first-hand model-comparison research.
exclusion_criteria: General LLM education papers without simulated student responses or student-behavior emulation.
Papers focused only on LLMs as tutors, graders, or feedback providers without generated student behavior.
Secondary summaries and non-primary paper pages unless used only for discovery.
Fine-tuning-only papers outside student simulation or misconception/error realism.
dedupe_method: Title, DOI, arXiv identifier, and ACL Anthology identifier were used to deduplicate landing pages and PDFs.

screening_decisions.title: Simulating Students or Sycophantic Problem Solving? On Misconception Faithfulness of LLM Simulators
screening_decisions.reason: Directly evaluates prompted off-the-shelf simulated students across seven models and identifies a model-choice-relevant misconception-faithfulness failure mode.

screening_decisions.title: Towards Valid Student Simulation with Large Language Models
screening_decisions.reason: Provides a validity framework and reporting checklist directly relevant to desk-based model-choice criteria and overclaiming controls.

screening_decisions.title: Can LLMs Effectively Simulate Human Learners? Teachers' Insights from Tutoring LLM Students
screening_decisions.reason: Adds full-text peer-reviewed teacher-observed authenticity evidence for LLM-simulated student behavior gaps.

screening_decisions.title: Malrulelib: Large-Scale Executable Misconception Reasoning With Step Traces For Modeling Student Thinking In Mathematics
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to misconception modeling, but the current pass already included the more directly applicable misconception-faithfulness simulator paper.

screening_decisions.title: Beagle: Behavior-Enforced Agent For Grounded Learner Emulation
screening_decisions.reason: Potentially relevant to grounded learner emulation, but deeper extraction was deferred to avoid expanding beyond the model-choice memo update.

screening_decisions.title: Check My Work? Measuring Sycophancy in a Simulated Educational Context
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to LLM educational sycophancy but focused on LLM assistant behavior rather than generated student responses.

screening_decisions.title: Invisible Saboteurs: Sycophantic LLMs Mislead Novices in Problem-Solving Tasks
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to learner interactions with LLMs, but not a synthetic student-response generation study.

screening_decisions.title: Secondary paper pages and discovery indexes
screening_decisions.reason: Used only for discovery; primary arXiv and ACL Anthology pages were used for extraction.

screening_summary: This targeted literature-only update searched for model-choice and student-simulation validity evidence that could refine the application memo without first-hand model benchmarking. It included one empirical model-limit paper, one conceptual validity framework, and one peer-reviewed teacher-authenticity study. The included evidence supports a stronger desk-based caution: off-the-shelf prompt-only models may produce fluent but invalid learner behavior, so model choice should be documented as implementation rationale rather than empirical superiority.
limitations: The pass was targeted rather than exhaustive.
The included empirical evidence is not automated scorer validation.
Do et al. is a recent arXiv preprint and should be monitored for venue status or revision.
Several adjacent sycophancy or learner-emulation papers were deferred or excluded because they were less directly tied to generated student responses.
